questions.promptTex: \text{Two parallel lines cut by a transversal. Corresponding angles are:}
hints: Corresponding angles are in the same position at each intersection.
When the lines are parallel they match up exactly.
questions.explanation: Corresponding angles (same position relative to each intersection) are **equal** when the two cut lines are parallel.

hints: They're on the same side of the transversal, between the two parallel lines.
They sum to 180°.
questions.explanation: Co-interior angles are **supplementary** — they sum to 180°.

questions.promptTex: \text{One angle in a parallel-lines + transversal setup is } 70°. \\ \text{What is the measure of its co-interior angle?}
hints: Co-interior pairs are supplementary.

questions.promptTex: \text{Two lines are parallel; a transversal makes alternate interior angles of} \\ 4x + 10 \text{ and } 6x - 30. \text{ Find } x.
hints: Alternate interior angles are equal when lines are parallel.
Set $4x + 10 = 6x - 30$ and solve.
questions.explanation: Alternate interior angles are equal: $4x + 10 = 6x - 30 \Rightarrow 40 = 2x \Rightarrow x = 20$.

steps.text: **Approach.** Alternate interior angles are equal when lines are parallel.
**Solution.** Alternate interior angles are equal: $4x + 10 = 6x - 30 \Rightarrow 40 = 2x \Rightarrow x = 20$.

questions.promptTex: \text{If two lines are cut by a transversal and a pair of corresponding angles are equal, then the lines are:}
hints: This is the converse of the corresponding-angle rule.
Equal corresponding angles ↔ parallel lines.
questions.explanation: If corresponding angles are equal, the two lines must be **parallel**. This converse is how we *prove* lines parallel from angle measurements.

questions.promptTex: \text{Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal.} \\ \text{One pair of corresponding angles is } 65° \text{ and } x°. \\ \text{Find } x.
hints: Corresponding angles are equal when lines are parallel.
steps: When two parallel lines are cut by a transversal, **corresponding angles** are equal.

questions.promptTex: \text{Parallel lines cut by a transversal: alternate interior angles are} \\ (3x - 10)° \text{ and } (x + 50)°. \text{ Find } x.
hints: Alternate interior angles are equal.
steps: Alternate interior angles are **equal** when the lines are parallel.

questions.promptTex: \text{Parallel lines + transversal: co-interior (same-side interior) angles} \\ \text{are } (2x + 20)° \text{ and } 70°. \text{ Find } x.
hints: Co-interior angles are SUPPLEMENTARY (sum to 180°).
steps: Co-interior (same-side interior) angles are **supplementary** when lines are parallel.

questions.promptTex: \text{Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One acute angle formed} \\ \text{is } 40°. \text{ How many of the 8 angles formed equal } 40°?
hints: Each transversal-cut creates 4 angles, two of $40°$ and two of $140°$.
Both intersections produce the same set, so 4 angles are $40°$ and 4 are $140°$.
questions.explanation: When parallel lines are cut by a transversal, half the 8 angles are equal to the original $40°$, the other half are $140°$.
steps: A transversal cuts each parallel line at 4 angles: 2 of $40°$ and 2 of $140°$.
Because the lines are parallel, both cuts produce the same angle set.

questions.promptTex: \text{Two parallel lines cut by a transversal. The angle on one side is } (4x - 5)°. \\ \text{The corresponding angle on the other parallel is } (3x + 20)°. \\ \text{Find the angle (in degrees).}
hints: Corresponding angles equal: $4x - 5 = 3x + 20$. Find $x$.
Plug back to get the angle.
steps: Plug back to find the angle: $4(25) - 5 = 100 - 5 = 95°$.

questions.promptTex: \text{Which pair of angles is NOT necessarily congruent when} \\ \text{two parallel lines are cut by a transversal?}
hints: Co-interior angles are supplementary (sum to 180°), not equal.
The other three are equal.
questions.explanation: Co-interior (same-side interior) angles are supplementary, not equal — they only happen to be equal when both are $90°$.
steps: (They'd only be equal if both happened to be $90°$.)

questions.promptTex: \text{Three parallel lines } \ell_1, \ell_2, \ell_3 \text{ are cut by a transversal.} \\ \text{Angle at } \ell_1 \text{ is } 75°. \text{ The corresponding angle at } \ell_3 \text{ is what?}
hints: All three lines are parallel — corresponding angles all equal.
questions.explanation: Corresponding angles between any pair of parallel lines are equal. So all three intersection points have the corresponding angle = $75°$.
steps: Parallel lines cut by a transversal create equal corresponding angles at every intersection.
If $\ell_1$ and $\ell_3$ are parallel (both parallel to $\ell_2$), the corresponding angles at $\ell_1$ and $\ell_3$ are equal.
